essentialCommands.name: banadd
essentialCommands.description: Adds a new ban rule on the selected virtual server. All parameters are   optional but at least one of the following must be set: ip, name, or uid.

essentialCommands.name: banclient
essentialCommands.description: Bans the client specified with ID clid from the server. Please note that this   will create two separate ban rules for the targeted clients IP address and   his unique identifier.

essentialCommands.name: bandel
essentialCommands.description: Deletes the ban rule with ID banid from the server.

essentialCommands.name: bandelall
essentialCommands.description: Deletes all active ban rules from the server.

essentialCommands.name: banlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of active bans on the selected virtual server.
examples: banid=7 ip=1.2.3.4 created=1259444002242 invokername=WolverinDEV invokercldbid=56 invokeruid=oHhi9WzXLNEFQOwAu4JYKGU+C+c= reason enforcements=0

essentialCommands.name: bantriggerlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of trigger events for a ban entry
examples: banid=1 serverid=1 client_unique_identifier=xxjnc14LmvTk+Lyrm8OOeo4tOqw= client_hardware_identifier=a282e3dc4cb8583c6d8e3466f38c1970 client_nickname=WolverinDEV connection_client_ip=127.0.0.1 timestamp=0|client_unique_identifier=xxjnc14LmvTk+Lyrm8OOeo4tOqw= client_hardware_identifier=a282e3dc4cb8583c6d8e3466f38c1970 client_nickname=WolverinDEV connection_client_ip=127.0.0.1 timestamp=0

essentialCommands.name: bindinglist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of IP addresses used by the server instance on multi-homed   machines. If no subsystem is specified, "voice" is used by default.

essentialCommands.name: channeladdperm
essentialCommands.description: Adds a set of specified permissions to a channel. Multiple permissions can be   added by providing the two parameters of each permission. A permission can be   specified by permid or permsid.

essentialCommands.name: channelclientaddperm
essentialCommands.description: Adds a set of specified permissions to a client in a specific channel.   Multiple permissions can be added by providing the three parameters of each   permission. A permission can be specified by permid or permsid.
examples: channelclientaddperm cid=12 cldbid=3 permid=17276 permvalue=50|permid=21415 permvalue=20

essentialCommands.name: channelclientdelperm
essentialCommands.description: Removes a set of specified permissions from a client in a specific channel.   Multiple permissions can be removed at once. A permission can be specified by   permid or permsid.

essentialCommands.name: channelclientpermlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of permissions defined for a client in a specific channel.
examples: cid=12 cldbid=3 permid=4353 permvalue=1 permnegated=0 permskip=0|permid=17276 permvalue=50 permnegated=0 permskip=0|permid=21415 ...

essentialCommands.name: channelcreate
essentialCommands.description: Creates a new channel using the given properties and displays its ID. Note   that this command accepts multiple properties which means that you're able to   specifiy all settings of the new channel at once.   For detailed information, see Channel Properties.

essentialCommands.name: channeldelete
essentialCommands.description: Deletes an existing channel by ID. If force is set to 1, the channel will be   deleted even if there are clients within. The clients will be kicked to the   default channel with an appropriate reason message.

essentialCommands.name: channeldelperm
essentialCommands.description: Removes a set of specified permissions from a channel. Multiple permissions   can be removed at once. A permission can be specified by permid or permsid.

essentialCommands.name: channeledit
essentialCommands.description: Changes a channels configuration using given properties. Note that this   command accepts multiple properties which means that you're able to change   all settings of the channel specified with cid at once.   For detailed information, see Channel Properties.

essentialCommands.name: channelfind
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of channels matching a given name pattern.

essentialCommands.name: channelgroupadd
essentialCommands.description: Creates a new channel group using a given name and displays its ID. The   optional type parameter can be used to create template groups.   For detailed information, see Definitions.

essentialCommands.name: channelgroupaddperm
essentialCommands.description: Adds a set of specified permissions to a channel group. Multiple permissions   can be added by providing the two parameters of each permission. A permission   can be specified by permid or permsid.

essentialCommands.name: channelgroupclientlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays all the client and/or channel IDs currently assigned to channel   groups. All three parameters are optional so you're free to choose the most   suitable combination for your requirements.
examples: cid=2 cldbid=9 cgid=9|cid=2 cldbid=24 cgid=9|cid=2 cldbid=47 cgid=9

essentialCommands.name: channelgroupcopy
essentialCommands.description: Creates a copy of the channel group specified with scgid. If tcgid is set to   0, the server will create a new group. To overwrite an existing group, simply   set tcgid to the ID of a designated target group. If a target group is set,   the name parameter will be ignored. The type parameter can be used to create   template groups.   For detailed information, see Definitions.

essentialCommands.name: channelgroupdel
essentialCommands.description: Deletes a channel group by ID. If force is set to 1, the channel group will   be deleted even if there are clients within.

essentialCommands.name: channelgroupdelperm
essentialCommands.description: Removes a set of specified permissions from the channel group. Multiple   permissions can be removed at once. A permission can be specified by permid   or permsid.

essentialCommands.name: channelgrouplist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of channel groups available on the selected virtual server.

essentialCommands.name: channelgrouppermlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of permissions assigned to the channel group specified with   cgid. If the -permsid option is specified, the output will contain the   permission names instead of the internal IDs.

essentialCommands.name: channelgrouprename
essentialCommands.description: Changes the name of a specified channel group.

essentialCommands.name: channelinfo
essentialCommands.description: Displays detailed configuration information about a channel including ID,   topic, description, etc.   For detailed information, see Channel Properties.

essentialCommands.name: channellist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of channels created on a virtual server including their ID,   order, name, etc. The output can be modified using several command options.

essentialCommands.name: channelmove
essentialCommands.description: Moves a channel to a new parent channel with the ID cpid. If order is   specified, the channel will be sorted right under the channel with the   specified ID. If order is set to 0, the channel will be sorted right below   the new parent.

essentialCommands.name: channelpermlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of permissions defined for a channel.
examples: cid=2 permid=4353 permvalue=1 permnegated=0 permskip=0|permid=17276 permvalue=50 ...

essentialCommands.name: clientaddperm
essentialCommands.description: Adds a set of specified permissions to a client. Multiple permissions can be   added by providing the three parameters of each permission. A permission can   be specified by permid or permsid.
examples: clientaddperm cldbid=16 permid=17276 permvalue=50 permskip=1|permid=21415 permvalue=20 permskip=0

essentialCommands.name: clientdbdelete
essentialCommands.description: Deletes a clients properties from the database.

essentialCommands.name: clientdbedit
essentialCommands.description: Changes a clients settings using given properties.   For detailed information, see Client Properties.

essentialCommands.name: clientdbfind
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of client database IDs matching a given pattern. You can   either search for a clients last known nickname or his unique identity by   using the -uid option.   		The pattern parameter can include regular characters and SQL wildcard   characters (e.g. %).

essentialCommands.name: clientdbinfo
essentialCommands.description: Displays detailed database information about a client including unique ID,   creation date, etc.

essentialCommands.name: clientdblist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of client identities known by the server including their   database ID, last nickname, etc.

essentialCommands.name: clientdelperm
essentialCommands.description: Removes a set of specified permissions from a client. Multiple permissions   can be removed at once. A permission can be specified by permid or permsid.

essentialCommands.name: clientedit
essentialCommands.description: Changes a clients or music bots settings using given properties.   For detailed information, see Client Properties.

essentialCommands.name: clientfind
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of clients matching a given name pattern.

essentialCommands.name: clientgetdbidfromuid
essentialCommands.description: Displays the database ID matching the unique identifier specified by cluid.

essentialCommands.name: clientgetids
essentialCommands.description: Displays all client IDs matching the unique identifier specified by cluid.

essentialCommands.name: clientgetnamefromdbid
essentialCommands.description: Displays the unique identifier and nickname matching the database ID   specified by cldbid.

essentialCommands.name: clientgetnamefromuid
essentialCommands.description: Displays the database ID and nickname matching the unique identifier   specified by cluid.

essentialCommands.name: clientgetuidfromclid
essentialCommands.description: Displays the unique identifier matching the clientID specified by clid.

essentialCommands.name: clientinfo
essentialCommands.description: Displays detailed configuration information about a client including unique   ID, nickname, client version, etc.

essentialCommands.name: clientkick
essentialCommands.description: Kicks one or more clients specified with clid from their currently joined   channel or from the server, depending on reasonid. The reasonmsg parameter   specifies a text message sent to the kicked clients. This parameter is   optional and may only have a maximum of 40 characters.   For detailed information, see Definitions.

essentialCommands.name: clientlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of clients online on a virtual server including their ID,   nickname, status flags, etc. The output can be modified using several command   options.   Please note that the output will only contain clients which are currently in   channels you're able to subscribe to.
examples: clid=5 cid=7 client_database_id=40 client_nickname=ScP client_type=0 client_away=1 client_away_message=not\shere|clid=6 ...

essentialCommands.name: clientmove
essentialCommands.description: Moves one or more clients specified with clid to the channel with ID cid. If   the target channel has a password, it needs to be specified with cpw. If the   channel has no password, the parameter can be omitted.

essentialCommands.name: clientpermlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of permissions defined for a client.
examples: cldbid=2 permid=4353 permvalue=1 permnegated=0 permskip=0|permid=17276 permvalue=50 permnegated=0 permskip=0|permid=21415 ...

essentialCommands.name: clientpoke
essentialCommands.description: Sends a poke message to the client specified with clid.

essentialCommands.name: clientsetserverquerylogin
essentialCommands.description: Updates your own ServerQuery login credentials using a specified username.   The password will be auto-generated.

essentialCommands.name: clientupdate
essentialCommands.description: Change your ServerQuery clients settings using given properties.   For detailed information, see Client Properties.

essentialCommands.name: complainadd
essentialCommands.description: Submits a complaint about a connected client with database ID tcldbid to the   server.

essentialCommands.name: complaindel
essentialCommands.description: Deletes the complaint about the client with ID tcldbid submitted by the   client with ID fcldbid from the server.

essentialCommands.name: complaindelall
essentialCommands.description: Deletes all complaints about the client with database ID tcldbid from the   server.

essentialCommands.name: complainlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of complaints on the selected virtual server. If tcldbid is   specified, only complaints about the targeted client will be shown.
examples: tcldbid=3 tname=Julian fcldbid=56 fname=Sven message=Bad\sguy! timestamp=1259440948 ...

essentialCommands.name: conversationfetch
essentialCommands.description: Fetches the newest message timestamps

essentialCommands.name: conversationhistory
usage: conversationhistory cid={channel id} [cpw={channel password}] [timestamp_begin={milliseconds since epoch}] [timestamp_end={milliseconds since epoch}] [message_count={max messages to receive}] [-merge]
essentialCommands.description: Queries the channel conversation history from 'timestamp_end' up to 'timestamp_begin'.

essentialCommands.name: custominfo
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of custom properties for the client specified with cldbid.

essentialCommands.name: customsearch
essentialCommands.description: Searches for custom client properties specified by ident and value. The value   parameter can include regular characters and SQL wildcard characters (e.g. %).

essentialCommands.name: ftcreatedir
essentialCommands.description: Creates new directory in a channels file repository.

essentialCommands.name: ftdeletefile
essentialCommands.description: Deletes one or more files stored in a channels file repository.

essentialCommands.name: ftgetfileinfo
essentialCommands.description: Displays detailed information about one or more specified files stored in a   channels file repository.
examples: ftgetfileinfo cid=2 cpw= path=\/Pic1.PNG|cid=3 cpw=Hello\sWorld path=\/Pic2.PNG
cid=2 path=\/ name=Stuff size=0 empty=0 datetime=1259415210 type=0|name=Pic1.PNG empty=0 size=563783 datetime=1259425462 type=1|name=Pic2.PNG empty=0 ...

essentialCommands.name: ftgetfilelist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of files and directories stored in the specified channels   file repository.
examples: cid=2 path=\/ name=Stuff size=0 datetime=1259415210 type=0|name=Pic1.PNG size=563783 datetime=1259425462 type=1|name=Pic2.PNG ...

essentialCommands.name: ftinitdownload
essentialCommands.description: Initializes a file transfer download. clientftfid is an arbitrary ID to   identify the file transfer on client-side. On success, the server generates a   new ftkey which is required to start downloading the file through TeaSpeak's   or TeamSpeak 3's file transfer interface.
examples: ftinitdownload clientftfid=1 name=\/image.iso cid=5 cpw= seekpos=0
clientftfid=1 serverftfid=7 ftkey=NrOga\/4d2GpYC5oKgxuclTO37X83ca\/1 port=30033 size=673460224 proto=0

essentialCommands.name: ftinitupload
essentialCommands.description: Initializes a file transfer upload. clientftfid is an arbitrary ID to   identify the file transfer on client-side. On success, the server generates a   new ftkey which is required to start uploading the file through TeaSpeak's   or TeamSpeak 3's file transfer interface.
examples: ftinitupload clientftfid=1 name=\/image.iso cid=5 cpw= size=673460224 overwrite=1 resume=0
clientftfid=1 serverftfid=6 ftkey=itRNdsIOvcBiBg\/Xj4Ge51ZSrsShHuid port=30033 seekpos=0 proto=0

essentialCommands.name: ftlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of running file transfers on the selected virtual server. The   output contains the path to which a file is uploaded to, the current transfer   rate in bytes per second, etc.
examples: clid=2 path=files\/virtualserver_1\/channel_5 name=image.iso size=673460224 sizedone=450756 clientftfid=2 serverftfid=6 sender=0 status=1 current_speed=60872.8 ...

essentialCommands.name: ftrenamefile
essentialCommands.description: Renames a file in a channels file repository. If the two parameters tcid and   tcpw are specified, the file will be moved into another channels file   repository.
examples: ftrenamefile cid=2 cpw= tcid=3 tcpw=secret oldname=\/Pic3.PNG newname=\/Pic3.PNG

essentialCommands.name: ftstop
essentialCommands.description: Stops the running file transfer with server-side ID serverftfid.

essentialCommands.name: gm
essentialCommands.description: Sends a text message to all clients on all virtual servers in the TeaSpeak   Server instance.

essentialCommands.name: hostinfo
essentialCommands.description: Displays detailed connection information about the server instance including   uptime, number of virtual servers online, traffic information, etc.      For detailed information, see Server Instance Properties.

essentialCommands.name: instanceedit
essentialCommands.description: Changes the server instance configuration using given properties.   For detailed information, see Server Instance Properties.

essentialCommands.name: instanceinfo
essentialCommands.description: Displays the server instance configuration including database revision   number, the file transfer port, default group IDs, etc.   For detailed information, see Server Instance Properties.

essentialCommands.name: listfeaturesupport
essentialCommands.description: List all supported features by the server.   This could be used to determine if a server has some functionality already   and so enable it for the user.
examples: name=error-bulks support=1 version=1 | name=advanced-channel-chat support=1 version=1 | ...

essentialCommands.name: logadd
essentialCommands.description: Writes a custom entry into the servers log. Depending on your permissions,   you'll be able to add entries into the server instance log and/or your   virtual servers log.

essentialCommands.name: login
essentialCommands.description: Authenticates with the TeaSpeak Server instance using given ServerQuery   login credentials.

essentialCommands.name: logout
essentialCommands.description: Deselects the active virtual server and logs out from the server instance.

essentialCommands.name: logquery
usage: logquery [begin={milliseconds since epoch}] [end={milliseconds since epoch}] [limit={entry count} [groups={server,channel,...}] [instance={0|1}]
essentialCommands.description: Displays a specified number of entries from the servers log. If instance is   set to 1, the server will return entries from the instance log.   Available groups are  custom, server, channel, permission, client, files and query.

essentialCommands.name: messageadd
essentialCommands.description: Sends an offline message to the client specified by cluid.

essentialCommands.name: messagedel
essentialCommands.description: Deletes an existing offline message with ID msgid from your inbox.

essentialCommands.name: messageget
essentialCommands.description: Displays an existing offline message with ID msgid from your inbox. Please   note that this does not automatically set the flag_read property of the   message.

essentialCommands.name: messagelist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of offline messages you've received. The output contains the   senders unique identifier, the messages subject, etc.

essentialCommands.name: messageupdateflag
essentialCommands.description: Updates the flag_read property of the offline message specified with msgid.   If flag is set to 1, the message will be marked as read.

essentialCommands.name: musicbotcreate
essentialCommands.description: Creates a new musicbot. The channel id is optional and   requires sufficient join power for the target channel.

essentialCommands.name: musicbotdelete
essentialCommands.description: Deletes a musicbot. The bot id is required and can be   aquired with the clientlist command (property: client_type=4).

essentialCommands.name: musicbotplayeraction
essentialCommands.description: Executes a action on a certain musicbot

essentialCommands.name: musicbotplayerinfo
essentialCommands.description: Aquires information about a musicbot.   This will give you detailed informations   about the playing status, current title, etc.   (See the examples below)

essentialCommands.name: musicbotplaylistassign
essentialCommands.description: Changes/sets the current playlist for a music bot

essentialCommands.name: permfind
essentialCommands.description: Displays detailed information about all assignments of the permission   specified with permid. The output is similar to permoverview which includes   the type and the ID of the client, channel or group associated with the   permission. A permission can be specified by permid or permsid.   Each result bulk contains the following entries:

essentialCommands.name: permget
essentialCommands.description: Displays the current value of the permission specified with permid or permsid   for your own connection. This can be useful when you need to check your own   privileges.

essentialCommands.name: permidgetbyname
essentialCommands.description: Displays the database ID of one or more permissions specified by permsid.

essentialCommands.name: permissionlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of permissions available on the server instance including ID,   name and description.

essentialCommands.name: permoverview
essentialCommands.description: Displays all permissions assigned to a client for the channel specified with   cid. If permid is set to 0, all permissions will be displayed. A permission   can be specified by permid or permsid.

essentialCommands.name: permreset
essentialCommands.description: Restores the default permission settings on the selected virtual server and   creates a new initial administrator token. Please note that in case of an   error during the permreset call - e.g. when the database has been modified or   corrupted - the virtual server will be deleted from the database.

essentialCommands.name: playlistaddperm
usage: playlistaddperm playlist_id={playlist_id} permsid={name} permvalue={value} permnegated={flag negated} permskip={flag skip}
essentialCommands.description: Add or modify a permission
examples: playlistaddperm playlist_id=13 permsid=i_playlist_needed_delete_power permvalue=200 permnegated=0 permskip=0

essentialCommands.name: playlistclientaddperm
usage: playlistclientaddperm playlist_id={playlist_id} cldbid={database_id} permsid={name} permvalue={value} permnegated={flag negated} permskip={flag skip}
essentialCommands.description: Add or modify a permission for a specific client
examples: playlistaddperm playlist_id=13 cldbid=2 permsid=i_playlist_delete_power permvalue=200 permnegated=0 permskip=0

essentialCommands.name: playlistclientlist
essentialCommands.description: Received all client database ids which have special permission set   for the playlist.

essentialCommands.name: playlistclientpermlist
essentialCommands.description: View all permissions, given to a playlist for a specific client.   A playlist owner ignores this permission and could view them anyways.
examples: playlist_id=13 cldbid=2 permid=253 permvalue=100 permnegated=0 permskip=0|...

essentialCommands.name: playlistcreate
essentialCommands.description: Creates a new playlist, which is directly owned by the invoker.   The owner has any rights, ignoring any permission given to the playlist.

essentialCommands.name: playlistdelclientperm
essentialCommands.description: Removes a permission from the playlist for a specific client

essentialCommands.name: playlistdelete
essentialCommands.description: Deletes a playlist and all their songs

essentialCommands.name: playlistdelperm
essentialCommands.description: Removes a permission from the playlist

essentialCommands.name: playlistedit
essentialCommands.description: Edit the properties for a specific playlist.   For detailed information, see playlist properties.

essentialCommands.name: playlistinfo
essentialCommands.description: Returns all playlist properties and their values
examples: playlist_id=13 playlist_title=Yet\sanother\splaylist playlist_description playlist_type=1 playlist_owner_dbid=1 playlist_owner_name=Hello\sWorld playlist_flag_delete_played=1 playlist_flag_finished=0 playlist_replay_mode=0 playlist_current_song_id=0

essentialCommands.name: playlistlist
essentialCommands.description: List all available and viewable playlists.   Own playlists are shown, ignoring the view power
examples: playlist_id=10 playlist_title=Yet\sanother\splaylist playlist_type=0 playlist_owner_dbid=1 playlist_owner_name=owned\svia\sbot needed_power_modify=-2 needed_power_permission_modify=-2 needed_power_delete=-2 needed_power_song_add=-2 needed_power_song_move=-2 needed_power_song_remove=-2|...

essentialCommands.name: playlistpermlist
essentialCommands.description: View all permissions, given to a playlist.   A playlist owner ignores this permission and could view them anyways.

essentialCommands.name: playlistsongadd
essentialCommands.description: Adds a new song entry to the playlist.   Playlists (e.g. from YouTube) will be resolved automatically!)

essentialCommands.name: playlistsonglist
essentialCommands.description: List all songs which are within a playlist.   When "-extract-metadata" is given, the fixed song_metadata content will   be given as normal parameters. These will begin with "song_metadata_".
examples: playlist_id=13 song_id=1 song_invoker=1 song_previous_song_id=0 song_url=https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/watch?v=XU8iCAxAsH4 song_url_loader song_loaded=0 song_metadata

essentialCommands.name: playlistsongremove
essentialCommands.description: Removes a song from the playlist

essentialCommands.name: playlistsongreorder
usage: playlistsongreorder playlist_id={playlist_id} song_id={song id} song_previous_song_id={order id}
essentialCommands.description: Moves a song and changes his position

essentialCommands.name: playlistsongsetcurrent
essentialCommands.description: Sets the current song which will be replayed.   When the playlist is bound to an bot the bot will abort the replay start start over again.

essentialCommands.name: propertylist
essentialCommands.description: Get a full list of all available properties
examples: flag_set=.... name=virtualserver_id flags=256 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_unique_identifier flags=392 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_name flags=408 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_welcomemessage flags=408 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_platform flags=256 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_version flags=256 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_maxclients flags=408 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_password flags=24 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_clientsonline flags=128 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_channelsonline flags=128 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_queryclientsonline flags=128 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_created flags=280 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_uptime flags=128 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_codec_encryption_mode flags=408 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_keypair flags=24 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_hostmessage flags=408 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_hostmessage_mode flags=408 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_default_server_group flags=408 type=SERVER|name=virtualserver_default_channel_group flags=408

essentialCommands.name: querychangepassword
essentialCommands.description: Change the password for the given query.   You could generate a new one or change the password to a specific one.

essentialCommands.name: querycreate
usage: querycreate client_login_name={username} [client_login_password={password}] [server_id={bounded server id}] [cldbid={client database id}]
essentialCommands.description: Creates a new query account with your own identity.

essentialCommands.name: querydelete
essentialCommands.description: Deletes a query account.

essentialCommands.name: querylist
essentialCommands.description: List all existing query accounts.   If no specific server is given, all accounts will be listed.   If the permission b_client_query_list isn't preset, all own accounts will be listed
examples: server_id=-1 flag_own=1 flag_all=1 client_unique_identifier=serveradmin client_login_name=serveradmin client_bounded_server=0|client_unique_identifier=serveradmin client_login_name=server_bot client_bounded_server=2|...
server_id=-1 flag_own=1 flag_all=1 client_unique_identifier=serveradmin client_login_name=server_bot client_bounded_server=2|...

essentialCommands.name: queryrename
essentialCommands.description: Renames a query account.   The new name must be free and not yet used!

essentialCommands.name: quit
essentialCommands.description: Closes the ServerQuery connection to the TeaSpeak Server instance.

essentialCommands.name: sendtextmessage
essentialCommands.description: Sends a text message to a specified target. If targetmode is set to 1, a   message is sent to the client with the ID specified by target. If targetmode   is set to 2 or 3, the target parameter will be ignored and a message is sent   to the current channel or server respectively.

essentialCommands.name: servercreate
essentialCommands.description: Creates a new virtual server using the given properties and displays its ID,   port and initial administrator privilege key. If virtualserver_port is not   specified, the server will test for the first unused UDP port.   The first virtual server will be running on UDP port 9987 by default.   Subsequently started virtual servers will be running on increasing UDP port   numbers.   For detailed information, see Virtual Server Properties.

essentialCommands.name: serverdelete
essentialCommands.description: Deletes the virtual server specified with sid. Please note that only virtual   servers in stopped state can be deleted.

essentialCommands.name: serveredit
essentialCommands.description: Changes the selected virtual servers configuration using given properties.    Note that this command accepts multiple properties which means that you're   able to change all settings of the selected virtual server at once.   For detailed information, see Virtual Server Properties.

essentialCommands.name: servergroupadd
essentialCommands.description: Creates a new server group using the name specified with name and displays   its ID. The optional type parameter can be used to create ServerQuery groups   and template groups. For detailed information, see Definitions.

essentialCommands.name: servergroupaddclient
essentialCommands.description: Adds a client to the server group specified with sgid. Please note that a   client cannot be added to default groups or template groups.

essentialCommands.name: servergroupaddperm
essentialCommands.description: Adds a set of specified permissions to the server group specified with sgid.   Multiple permissions can be added by providing the four parameters of each   permission. A permission can be specified by permid or permsid.
examples: servergroupaddperm sgid=13 permid=17276 permvalue=50 permnegated=0 permskip=0|permid=21415 permvalue=20 permnegated=0
servergroupaddperm sgid=3 permsid=b_virtualserver_modify_maxclients permvalue=1 permnegated=0 permskip=0

essentialCommands.name: servergroupautoaddperm
essentialCommands.description: Adds a set of specified permissions to *ALL* regular server groups on all   virtual servers. The target groups will be identified by the value of their   i_group_auto_update_type permission specified with sgtype. Multiple   permissions can be added at once. A permission can be specified by permid or   permsid.      The known values for sgtype are:      10: Channel Guest   15: Server Guest   20: Query Guest   25: Channel Voice   30: Server Normal   35: Channel Operator   40: Channel Admin   45: Server Admin   50: Query Admin
examples: servergroupautoaddperm sgtype=45 permsid=b_virtualserver_start permvalue=1 permskip=0 permnegated=0

essentialCommands.name: servergroupautodelperm
essentialCommands.description: Removes a set of specified permissions from *ALL* regular server groups on   all virtual servers. The target groups will be identified by the value of   their i_group_auto_update_type permission specified with sgtype. Multiple   permissions can be removed at once. A permission can be specified by permid   or permsid.      The known values for sgtype are:      10: Channel Guest   15: Server Guest   20: Query Guest   25: Channel Voice   30: Server Normal   35: Channel Operator   40: Channel Admin   45: Server Admin   50: Query Admin

essentialCommands.name: servergroupclientlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays the IDs of all clients currently residing in the server group   specified with sgid. If you're using the optional -names option, the output   will also contain the last known nickname and the unique identifier of the   clients.

essentialCommands.name: servergroupcopy
essentialCommands.description: Creates a copy of the server group specified with ssgid. If tsgid is set to   0, the server will create a new group. To overwrite an existing group, simply   set tsgid to the ID of a designated target group. If a target group is set,   the name parameter will be ignored.   The type parameter can be used to create ServerQuery groups and template   groups. For detailed information, see Definitions.

essentialCommands.name: servergroupdel
essentialCommands.description: Deletes the server group specified with sgid. If force is set to 1, the   server group will be deleted even if there are clients within.

essentialCommands.name: servergroupdelclient
essentialCommands.description: Removes a client specified with cldbid from the server group specified with   sgid.

essentialCommands.name: servergroupdelperm
essentialCommands.description: Removes a set of specified permissions from the server group specified with   sgid. Multiple permissions can be removed at once. A permission can be   specified by permid or permsid.

essentialCommands.name: servergrouplist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of server groups available. Depending on your permissions,   the output may also contain global ServerQuery groups and template groups.
examples: sgid=9 name=Server\sAdmin type=1 iconid=300 savedb=1|sgid=10 name=Normal type=1 iconid=0 savedb=1|sgid=11 ...

essentialCommands.name: servergrouppermlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of permissions assigned to the server group specified with   sgid. If the -permsid option is specified, the output will contain the   permission names instead of the internal IDs.

essentialCommands.name: servergrouprename
essentialCommands.description: Changes the name of the server group specified with sgid.

essentialCommands.name: servergroupsbyclientid
essentialCommands.description: Displays all server groups the client specified with cldbid is currently   residing in.

essentialCommands.name: serveridgetbyport
essentialCommands.description: Displays the database ID of the virtual server running on the UDP port   specified by virtualserver_port.

essentialCommands.name: serverinfo
essentialCommands.description: Displays detailed configuration information about the selected virtual server   including unique ID, number of clients online, configuration, etc.   For detailed information, see Virtual Server Properties.

essentialCommands.name: serverlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of virtual servers including their ID, status, number of   clients online, etc. If you're using the -all option, the server will list   all virtual servers stored in the database. This can be useful when multiple   server instances with different machine IDs are using the same database. The   machine ID is used to identify the server instance a virtual server is   associated with.   The status of a virtual server can be either online, offline, booting up,   shutting down or virtual online. While most of them are self-explanatory,   virtual online is a bit more complicated.   Whenever you select a virtual server which is currently stopped with the   -virtual parameter, it will be started in virtual mode which means you are   able to change its configuration, create channels or change permissions, but   no regular TeaSpeak or TeamSpeak 3 Client can connect. As soon as the last ServerQuery   client deselects the virtual server, its status will be changed back to   offline.

essentialCommands.name: servernotifyregister
essentialCommands.description: Registers for a specified category of events on a virtual server to receive   notification messages. Depending on the notifications you've registered for,   the server will send you a message on every event in the view of your   ServerQuery client (e.g. clients joining your channel, incoming text   messages, server configuration changes, etc). The event source is declared by   the event parameter while id can be used to limit the notifications to a   specific channel.

essentialCommands.name: servernotifyunregister
essentialCommands.description: Unregisters all events previously registered with servernotifyregister so you   will no longer receive notification messages.

essentialCommands.name: serverprocessstop
essentialCommands.description: Stops the entire TeaSpeak Server instance by shutting down the process.

essentialCommands.name: serverrequestconnectioninfo
essentialCommands.description: Displays detailed connection information about the selected virtual server   including uptime, traffic information, etc.

essentialCommands.name: serversnapshotcreate
essentialCommands.description: Displays a snapshot of the selected virtual server containing all settings,   groups and known client identities. The data from a server snapshot can be   used to restore a virtual servers configuration, channels and permissions   using the serversnapshotdeploy command.

essentialCommands.name: serversnapshotdeploy
essentialCommands.description: Restores the selected virtual servers configuration using the data from a   previously created server snapshot. Please note that the TeaSpeak Server   does NOT check for necessary permissions while deploying a snapshot so the   command could be abused to gain additional privileges.

essentialCommands.name: serverstart
essentialCommands.description: Starts the virtual server specified with sid. Depending on your permissions,   you're able to start either your own virtual server only or all virtual   servers in the server instance.

essentialCommands.name: serverstop
essentialCommands.description: Stops the virtual server specified with sid. Depending on your permissions,   you're able to stop either your own virtual server only or all virtual   servers in the server instance.

essentialCommands.name: servertemppasswordadd
essentialCommands.description: Sets a new temporary server password specified with pw. The temporary   password will be valid for the number of seconds specified with duration. The   client connecting with this password will automatically join the channel   specified with tcid. If tcid is set to 0, the client will join the default   channel.
examples: servertemppasswordadd pw=secret desc=none duration=3600 tcid=117535 tcpw=secret

essentialCommands.name: servertemppassworddel
essentialCommands.description: Deletes the temporary server password specified with pw.

essentialCommands.name: servertemppasswordlist
essentialCommands.description: Returns a list of active temporary server passwords. The output contains the   clear-text password, the nickname and unique identifier of the creating   client.
examples: nickname=serveradmin uid=serveradmin desc=none pw_clear=secret start=1331496494 end=1331500094 tcid=117535

essentialCommands.name: setclientchannelgroup
essentialCommands.description: Sets the channel group of a client to the ID specified with cgid.

essentialCommands.name: tokenactionlist
permissions: b_virtualserver_token_list_all for listing actions of not owned tokens
essentialCommands.description: Displays all actions the token does when applied.   Either "token_id" or "token" must be supplied to identify the token.   Lookup the tokens manual for more information.
examples: action_type=2 action_id=6 action_id1=10 action_id2=0 action_text|action_type=2 action_id=7 action_id1=2 ...

essentialCommands.name: tokenadd
essentialCommands.description: Create a new token with the defined characteristics
examples: tokenadd token_description=My\sDesc token_max_uses=1 action_type=2 action_id1=10 | action_type=2 action_id1=2 | action_type=2 action_id1=3

essentialCommands.name: tokendelete
essentialCommands.description: Delete a token

essentialCommands.name: tokenedit
essentialCommands.description: Edit an already existing token.   Add new actions or remove any action by only supplying the action id.

essentialCommands.name: tokenlist
essentialCommands.description: Displays a list of available tokens.   If "-own-only" has been passed or you dont have permissions to list all   tokens, only your tokens will be shown.

essentialCommands.name: tokenuse
essentialCommands.description: Use a token.

essentialCommands.name: use
essentialCommands.description: Selects the virtual server specified with sid or port to allow further   interaction. The ServerQuery client will appear on the virtual server and   acts like a real TeaSpeak or TeamSpeak 3 Client, except it's unable to send or receive   voice data.   If your database contains multiple virtual servers using the same UDP port,   use will select a random virtual server using the specified port.

essentialCommands.name: version
essentialCommands.description: Displays the servers version information including platform and build number.

essentialCommands.name: whoami
essentialCommands.description: Displays information about your current ServerQuery connection including your   loginname, etc.
